data.title: Quantum Computing News: Security Risks and Hardware Advances Start to Matter for Investors
data.description:
Welcome to Monday's update on quantum computing. As the year moves forward, attention is shifting from research milestones toward security planning, capital markets activity, and early commercial tools that already affect investors. As always, this update focuses on practical developments tied to quantum systems and the firms building around them.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In today's edition, we cover new progress in quantum simulation on classical hardware, rising concern around quantum-driven security risk, and continued movement across public and pre-public quantum companies.

Let us begin.

Classical Hardware Advances Support Quantum Research

First, MicroCloud Hologram Inc. (HOLO) is showing how classical hardware can still play a key role in quantum research. The company announced progress using field programmable gate arrays to speed up tensor network simulations used in quantum physics and spin models.

By moving key math tasks directly into custom hardware circuits, the system reduced memory strain and improved parallel work. Testing showed speeds about 70% faster than standard CPUs and more than double the energy efficiency. While this is not quantum hardware, it supports research teams that model quantum systems long before full-scale machines arrive.

As a result, tools like this may help shorten research cycles and lower costs for labs and firms working on quantum design.

Quantum Security Moves from Theory to Portfolio Risk

Next, quantum security is no longer just a technical topic. Jefferies recently removed Bitcoin (BTC) from a key Asia-focused portfolio due to long-term quantum risk. The firm cited the chance that future quantum systems could weaken the cryptography that protects digital assets.

While the timeline remains unclear, the concern is about the risk that cannot be reversed once it appears. This decision shows that large investors are now weighing quantum threats when shaping long-term asset exposure. At the same time, post-quantum encryption work continues as firms look to protect data created today from future attacks.

This shift places security planning alongside price and policy in portfolio reviews.

Post Quantum Chips Gain Real World Scale

Meanwhile, SEALSQ Corp. (LAES) is expanding its role in post quantum security through hardware. The company is already deploying secure chips that use quantum-safe cryptography in millions of devices worldwide.

These chips act as hardware roots of trust and aim to protect data against future decryption risks. Management has stressed that the threat is present now because data can be collected today and decoded later. As a result, firms are being urged to adopt protection well ahead of quantum breakthroughs.

This approach reflects a longer view of risk that matters for governments and large enterprises.

Leadership and Listings Signal Market Maturity

Several quantum firms are preparing for the next stage of growth. SEEQC Inc. announced plans to go public through a merger that values the firm at about $1 billion. The company builds control and readout chips that sit close to quantum processors and help reduce system delays.

In addition, firms like Quantinuum, IonQ (IONQ), Xanadu, PsiQuantum, and Infleqtion continue to add leaders with experience in defense, finance, security, and public markets. These moves suggest the industry is shifting from lab focus toward scale, regulation, and investor readiness.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up all the stocks appearing in the piece alongside quantum computing stocks. It's a quick way to see how they stack up and where the field could be heading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Looking for Exposure to Johnson & Johnson Stock (JNJ) ahead of Q4 Earnings? Here's How to Buy Without the Risk
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is set to report its Q4 earnings on January 21. Analysts expect revenue of about $24.16 billion, up 7.3% year‑over‑year, and earnings of $2.49 per share, reflecting a 22.1% increase from last year. With expectations running high, this could be the right moment for investors to seek exposure to JNJ's potential upside, particularly through exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that offer diversified exposure without the risk of holding the stock directly.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Investors seeking exposure to JNJ stock may consider iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF (IHE) and Portfolio Building Block World Pharma and Biotech Index ETF (PBPH). Let's take a deeper look at these two ETFs.

iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF (IHE)

The IHE ETF focuses specifically on large U.S. pharmaceutical companies. The fund invests in a focused group of major drug companies known for steady cash flow, solid pipelines, and stable performance even when markets are volatile. Importantly, JNJ stock accounts for 23% of the IHE ETF's total holdings.

Apart from JNJ, some of the top stocks in the IHE ETF are Eli Lilly (LLY), Merck (MRK), and Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY). Overall, the ETF has $971.66 million in assets under management (AUM). Also, it has an expense ratio of 0.38%. Over the past six months, the IHE ETF has gained 28.09%.

On TipRanks, IHE has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 52 Buys, two Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $102.17, the average IHE ETF price target implies 19.24% upside potential.

data.title: Insurance Giant AIG Teams up With CVC Capital Partners in $3.5B Investment Move
data.description:
U.S. insurance giant American International Group (AIG) has struck a partnership worth up to $3.5 billion with private equity group CVC Capital Partners.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wealthy Individuals Fund

Under the agreement, AIG will commit up to $2 billion to funds and separately managed accounts run by CVC, alongside up to $1.5 billion for a new evergreen fund targeted at wealthy individuals. The money will be invested in both private and liquid credit strategies.

CVC said the partnership would allow it to deploy long-dated insurance capital into diversified credit investments.

Rob Lucas, CEO of CVC, said: "Our partnership with AIG is a powerful endorsement of CVC's ability to serve the evolving needs of global insurance institutions at scale. The secondaries transaction provides a compelling foundation for our private equity evergreen secondaries vehicle, following on from the launch of our credit evergreen and private equity products last year."

Peter Zaffino, Chairman & CEO of AIG, said: "CVC is a highly respected, world-class global investment manager with deep capabilities across credit and private markets. This partnership marks our first collaboration with a European headquartered asset manager and supports our strategy of actively managing our investment portfolio."

Increasing Search for Capital

The deal is another signal of an increasing trend among alternative asset managers seeking stable capital sources as institutional fundraising slows. Firms including Blackstone (BX), Apollo (APO), KKR (KKR), and CVC have all expanded their insurance partnerships to support asset growth and fee stability.

For AIG, it means increased exposure to alternative strategies as it rebalances its investment portfolio. The insurer has already partnered with Blackstone through multiple asset management and reinsurance structures.

Is AIG a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, AIG has a hold consensus based on 2 Buy and 12 Hold ratings. Its highest price target is $96. AIG stock's consensus price target is $85.14, implying a 16.74% upside.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Tesla & Geely Group Stock Set to Rally as First Winners of Canada's EV Tariff Cut
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) and brands owned by the Geely Group (GELYF) are expected to be the primary winners after Canada decided to lower import taxes on electric vehicles made in China. This new trade deal allows 49,000 vehicles to enter the country each year with a much lower tax rate of 6.1 percent, completely removing the previous 100 percent penalty. In exchange for this change, China has agreed to lower its own trade barriers on Canadian canola.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla and Geely Gain Immediate Access

Because they already have vehicles that meet North American safety standards, Tesla, Volvo (VLVLY), and Polestar (PSNY) are in the best position to use this new quota. Before the high taxes were put in place in 2024, Tesla was a massive player in this space. The company shipped over 44,000 cars from China to Canada in 2023 alone. Now that the 100 percent tax is gone, these companies can start bringing in lower-cost models from their Chinese factories almost immediately.

To help more brands enter the market quickly, the Canadian government has promised to speed up its paperwork. Transport Canada will now certify new electric models from China in just eight weeks. This change is designed to help other big names like BYD (BYDDF) join the market. BYD is currently the largest EV maker in the world, but it has very few sales in Canada right now. The new rules could give their budget-friendly cars a huge advantage.

Luxury Brands See Major Price Cuts

The high-end market is also seeing a big shift. Lotus, a sports car brand owned by Geely, expects its prices to drop significantly. Their Eletre SUV, which usually costs over $300,000 in Canada, could see its price fall by about 50 percent thanks to the lower tax rate. This makes luxury electric cars much more competitive against local and European rivals.

The deal includes specific rules to make sure regular drivers benefit too. By the year 2030, half of all the cars allowed under this special quota must have a price tag of $35,000 or less. This is a major change because most electric vehicles currently for sale in Canada are much more expensive. The government hopes this will force all car companies to lower their prices to stay competitive.

Key Takeaway

To put it simply, Canada is trying to balance two very different needs. On one hand, they want to help their farmers sell canola to China. On the other hand, they want more people to be able to afford an electric car. These tax cuts make it much easier for companies like Tesla and Volvo to sell cars, but the policy also allows for more low-cost competition to enter the country. This plan represents a gamble that could make electric vehicles more common on Canadian roads, even if it creates more pressure for local factories.

Investors can compare the stocks mentioned in this article side-by-side on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Joby vs Archer: Confidence or Upside? Which eVTOL Stock Is Better for 2026
data.description:
Joby Aviation (JOBY) and Archer Aviation (ACHR) enter 2026 at very different points in the same race, as both are working toward a commercial electric air taxi service. However, the market is treating them very differently: Joby is being rewarded for steady progress and early revenue, while Archer is being valued for potential and optional paths. As a result, the debate for 2026 is simple. One stock offers higher confidence, and the other offers higher upside.

data.title: New World Development Stock Soars 16% as Parent Outlines 'Win-Win' Strategy
data.description:
Shares in troubled property developer and investor New World Development (0017) jumped 16% on Monday, reaching a peak not seen in two years. This came after its parent company stated plans to pursue "win-win" deals.

Henry Cheng Kar-shun, the chair of parent company Chow Tai Fook Enterprises, made the comment in an exclusive interview with the South China Morning Post. The comment has fueled optimism that fresh actions moves from the parent company may help to further improve the developer's finances.

The rally comes as New World Development, which develops residential, retail, office, and industrial properties as well as operates department stores, has managed to assuage investor concern about its debt profile. The company's shares climbed about 40% in 2025 and have rallied 52.27% since the start of this year alone.

data.title: The Stock Market Is Closed on MLK Day, but Here's What Goldman Sachs Says to Buy When Trading Resumes
data.description:
The stock market isn't open on Martin Luther King Jr. Day, but that doesn't mean investors are sitting idle. While markets are closed in observance of the holiday, Wall Street's focus is already shifting toward what could be top trades once trading resumes.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

One name drawing attention from Goldman Sachs analyst Asad Haider is BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX). On Friday, Haider upgraded the stock from Neutral to Buy and lifted his 12-month price target to $142 from $115, implying 30% upside from current levels.

Haider believes BioNTech sits "at the nexus of significant developments occurring in the oncology field," pointing to the company's expanding footprint across next-generation immuno-oncology and antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). In Haider's view, that positioning could give BioNTech a meaningful role in what the analyst describes as the "next wave" of cancer treatment innovation – well beyond the company's legacy COVID-19 vaccine business.

Importantly, the bullish call isn't built around a single asset or trial. Haider highlights BioNTech's broad, strategic portfolio, which includes novel PD-1/VEGF bispecifics, ADC programs, and mRNA-based cancer vaccines. The analyst argues this diversified approach allows the company to pursue multiple shots on goal across large oncology indications, while also reducing dependence on any single readout.

While COVID-related revenue continues to generate meaningful cash flow, Haider believes the oncology pipeline is set to become the more material driver of stock performance. The analyst notes that BioNTech shares have lagged in 2025, in part due to lingering overhang from the COVID franchise. However, he sees the narrative beginning to shift as the company moves deeper into late-stage development, with a series of clinical milestones expected to build into 2026.

In fact, Haider points to 2026 as the start of a multi-year catalyst-rich period, with BioNTech expecting Phase 2 and Phase 3 data readouts across its oncology programs. In his framework, success does not need to be binary; progress in individual indications could independently unlock value and steadily rebuild investor confidence.

Haider argues that BioNTech's current share price effectively values the company close to its cash and COVID business alone, assigning limited credit to its oncology assets. In his view, that creates what he describes as "asymmetric upside," particularly if upcoming data begin to validate the pipeline's breadth and potential.

"We believe BNTX has strategically positioned itself to be a leader in this next wave of potential advancements, presenting a unique risk/reward opportunity," the analyst summed up.

Overall, BNTX has picked up 15 recent analyst reviews, and these break down to 12 Buys and 3 Holds to support the Strong Buy consensus rating. The shares are priced at $109 and have an average target price of $141.93, which is practically the same as Haider's. (See BNTX stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel
